author: @MadRat70
text: Robert, you need a wider collection space for the swirl.  The tangential velocity of the swirl locks step with the inner/center of the swirl.  What may seem slow on the outside edge is deceiving because it is moving much greater distance around than at the center.  If you ever swung an object on a string around a circle, and then shortened the string, you see the effect.

replies.text: The type of generator Robert is demonstrating - as he correctly states at the beginning - is not about drop height of the water.  You post a simple question. author: @zenmanproject

replies.author: @DoubleBob

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: like a Tesla in reverse? the water flowing through what is usually the exhaust port ?

replies.author: @stevenelson886
replies.text:
@ThinkingandTinkering A Tesla turbine on it's side, exhaust downward. 

The volute would likely need to be narrowed to increase the velocity of the flow aimed at the tangent of the disks and increase the number of spirals of the fluid before it exits. Including the longer star washers (I think it's the 1921 patent) would be paramount to give it starting torque. The larger the diameter of the disks, the lower the rpm needs to be to handle a higher linear velocity at the tangent. That's the best way to scale a TT.

Interestingly, you could probably combine Tesla disks with the twisted impeller blades at the exhaust of the tesla disks. Try laser cut acrylic, should be easy enough to experiment with.

Don't forget the star washers. Most people on YT forget them and complain that Tesla turbines have no torque. They are important.
